localBiteReport: The most current reports available (within roughly the past week, from Captain Experiences reports out of Santa Rosa Beach and Shalimar) indicate that speckled trout are the standout inshore bite right now on Choctawhatchee Bay — described as plentiful and actively feeding despite some persistent wind pushing anglers to more sheltered areas. Redfish are also showing up well, including at least one report of a bull red over 40 inches landed near Santa Rosa Beach. A Shalimar-area guide noted the inshore bite had been tough but was improving daily as of about a week ago, suggesting conditions are trending in the right direction heading into mid-July. Water temps are sitting in the low 80s, which is typical for this time of year and generally favorable for trout and reds around grass flats and bridge structure. No recent firsthand reports on flounder, sheepshead, or black drum were found, though all three are seasonally present in the bay this time of year.
localBiteSource: Captain Experiences (captainexperiences.com) — Destin/Santa Rosa Beach/Shalimar guide reports, approximately 6–14 days old as of July 10, 2026 · Auto-refreshed via Claude API
